title: dacryodes macrophylla
intro: Dacryodes macrophylla, also known as the African pear or wild mango, is a tropical plant native to West and Central Africa. The plant's oil, extracted from its seeds, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dacryodes macrophylla oil include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the oil is often used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, as well as in capsules or tinctures for internal use.
